full_response:
We take c = 1 unless explicitly written.

1.  Proper period of Alice’s blinker  
   Δτ = 1 s                 (her rest frame)

2.  Lorentz factor for Bob  
   β = v/c = 0.8  
   γ = 1/√(1−β²) = 1/√(1−0.64) = 1/0.6 = 5/3 ≃ 1.6667.

3.  Interval between successive emissions in Bob’s inertial frame  
   (pure time–dilation effect)  
   Δt_emit = γ Δτ = (5/3)(1 s) = 5/3 s ≃ 1.667 s.

4.  Extra delay because Alice is receding  
   During Δt_emit Alice moves an additional distance  
   Δx = v Δt_emit = βc Δt_emit = 0.8 (5/3) c s = 4/3 ls.  
   Light from the second blink therefore has to cover an extra 4/3 ls, costing  
   Δt_extra = Δx/c = 4/3 s.

5.  Total interval between receptions in Bob’s frame  
   Δt_recv = Δt_emit + Δt_extra  
            = γ Δτ + βγ Δτ  
            = γ(1+β) Δτ  
            = (5/3)(1+0.8)(1 s)  
            = (5/3)(1.8) s  
            = 3 s.

Equivalently, using the relativistic Doppler formula supplied on the sheet  
 f_O = γ f_S (1 − β)  (here θ_S = 0)  
gives f_O = (5/3)(1 Hz)(1−0.8) = 1/3 Hz ⇒ Δt_recv = 1/f_O = 3 s, in agreement.

\boxed{\Delta t_{\text{Bob}} = 3 \text{ s}}

Confidence: 95%
